journal_entry.rationale: Recording utility expense incurred but not yet paid. Debit Utilities Expense (expense increase) to recognize the cost in the current period per accrual accounting. Credit Accounts Payable (liability increase) to record the obligation to pay the utility provider. No tax lines needed as utility bills typically include embedded taxes already in the stated amount.

output.reason: Accruing a utility bill means recognizing the expense now but not yet paying it. The credit side records the liability increase (Accounts Payable or Utilities Payable). The debit side (not classified here) would increase Utilities Expense.
